target.title: Bug: openclaw-weixin startAccount ???,?????????? session
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix manifest-only/read-only channel account resolution so route-safe account IDs can resolve config entries whose raw provider IDs contain opaque characters like `@` or `.`.
- Reuse the shared normalized account lookup path and keep blocked object keys out of normalized fallback matches.
- Add regression coverage for both the openclaw-weixin-style raw account key and the blocked-key default-account case.

## Linked context

Addresses #93556.

- Fix classification: root-cause bug fix, scoped to external channel/plugin account config resolution.

- Maintainer-ready confidence: high. The fix is covered by focused routing and read-only plugin contract tests, and the diff is limited to account lookup plus manifest channel account resolution.

- Root cause: manifest channel account listing normalized raw account keys for routing, but manifest channel account resolution used a different mapping contract by looking up the already-normalized route ID directly in the raw `accounts` object. A config key such as `59000514e8ad@im.bot` was listed as `59000514e8ad-im-bot`, but the resolver could not map that normalized ID back to the original raw config entry.

- Why this is root-cause fix: the resolver now uses the same normalized account ID contract as the lister, so raw provider IDs and route-safe IDs map through one resolver invariant. The shared normalized lookup also filters blocked raw keys during fallback matching, preserving the existing prototype-pollution guard at the lookup source instead of adding a downstream fallback.

- What did NOT change: no plugin manifest format, gateway startup behavior, account ID normalization rules, generated metadata, provider-specific openclaw-weixin code, or unrelated channel routing behavior changed.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: an external manifest-only channel can list a raw account key like `59000514e8ad@im.bot` as `59000514e8ad-im-bot`, then resolve that listed account ID back to the raw account config used by `startAccount`.

- Real environment tested: repository runtime source path with `node --import tsx`, using the actual `resolveNormalizedAccountEntry` helper and routing `normalizeAccountId` implementation after this patch.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
  import { resolveNormalizedAccountEntry } from './src/routing/account-lookup.ts';
  import { normalizeAccountId } from './src/routing/account-id.ts';

  const accounts = JSON.parse('{"59000514e8ad@im.bot":{"enabled":true,"baseUrl":"https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com"},"__proto__":{"enabled":true,"baseUrl":"blocked"}}');
  const resolved = resolveNormalizedAccountEntry(accounts, '59000514e8ad-im-bot', normalizeAccountId);
  const blockedDefault = resolveNormalizedAccountEntry(accounts, 'default', normalizeAccountId);
  console.log(JSON.stringify({
    listedRouteId: normalizeAccountId('59000514e8ad@im.bot'),
    resolvedEnabled: resolved?.enabled,
    resolvedBaseUrl: resolved?.baseUrl,
    blockedDefaultBaseUrl: blockedDefault?.baseUrl ?? null,
  }, null, 2));
  EOF
  ```

  Exit code: 0

- Evidence after fix: copied Node runtime console output from the command above.

  ```json
  {
    "listedRouteId": "59000514e8ad-im-bot",
    "resolvedEnabled": true,
    "resolvedBaseUrl": "https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com",
    "blockedDefaultBaseUrl": null
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the actual runtime source helper maps raw provider ID `59000514e8ad@im.bot` to route-safe ID `59000514e8ad-im-bot`, resolves that route-safe ID back to the raw account config including `enabled: true` and the configured `baseUrl`, and does not select the blocked `__proto__` entry as the default account.

- What was not tested: no live WeChat/openclaw-weixin service was contacted, and no real external credentials were used.

## Tests and validation

- Plugin test / extension-specific contract coverage: `src/channels/plugins/read-only.test.ts`
- Routing helper regression coverage: `src/routing/account-lookup.test.ts`
- Validation result: `2 passed (2)` test files, `36 passed (36)` tests.
- Pre-submit review: ClawSweeper commit review completed with `nothing_found`, highest severity `none`.

## Risk checklist

- [x] The diff is scoped to manifest channel account resolution and normalized account lookup.
- [x] Exact account keys still take precedence before normalized fallback matching.
- [x] Blocked object keys remain excluded from normalized fallback matching.
- [x] No dependency, schema, migration, generated metadata, or public plugin API changes.
- [x] No local paths, credentials, or private logs are included in this PR body.

## Current review state

Ready for maintainer review after local validation and ClawSweeper commit review passed on the current head.
